content.subtitle: Use
rows: To express a wish about the present or a general situation
Maria wishes she **lived** near the sea.
I wish I **wasn't** so nervous before exams.
To express regret about something that happened in the past
He wishes he **had listened** to his teacher's advice.
I wish we **hadn't stayed** out so late last night.
To express annoyance about someone's behaviour, or to complain
I wish the neighbours **would stop** making so much noise.
I wish you **wouldn't leave** your things everywhere.
To express wishes about what is possible now or in the future
She wishes she **could play** the piano.
I wish I **could stay** longer, but I have to leave.

content.subtitle: Extra notes
items: With wish + past simple, both was and were are acceptable for I/he/she/it. Were is the more formal option: I wish I was/were a better cook.
If only can replace wish with the same meaning: If only I was/were a better cook.

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: We don't use would when wishing for something about ourselves: I wish I knew the answer. (not I wish I would know the answer.)
For situations that might really happen in the future, use hope instead of wish: I hope the weather is nice tomorrow.

sections.title: A: Complete with the correct form
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence using wish and the verb in brackets.

items.sentence: I wish I ...... (have) more free time.
Sam wishes he ...... (not be) so shy.
We wish we ...... (can) speak Japanese.
She wishes she ...... (study) harder for the exam.
I wish my neighbours ...... (not play) loud music at night.
Tom wishes he ...... (not forget) his umbrella yesterday.
I wish I ...... (be) taller.
Mia wishes she ...... (can come) with us next weekend.

sections.title: B: Choose the correct option
sections.instructions: Choose the best option for each sentence.

items.sentence: I wish I **know / knew / had known** the answer now.
They wish they **wouldn't be / weren't / hadn't been** late every day.
They wish they **can / could / had been able** drive.
I wish you **stop / would stop / had stopped** shouting.
She wishes she **saw / had seen / would see** the film last night.
If only they **are / were / had been** better at maths.

sections.title: C: Rewrite with wish or if only
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence so it keeps the same meaning.

items.sentence: I don't have a bike. → ......
items.answer: I wish I had a bike.

items.sentence: My brother never tidies his room. → ......
items.answer: I wish my brother would tidy his room.

items.sentence: I didn't bring my dictionary yesterday. → ......
items.answer: I wish I had brought my dictionary yesterday.

items.sentence: I'm not allowed to stay out late. → ......
items.answer: I wish I could stay out late.

items.sentence: It may snow tomorrow (possible). → ......
items.answer: I hope it doesn't snow tomorrow.

items.sentence: I'm not an astronaut. → ......
items.answer: If only I was an astronaut./If only I were an astronaut.

sections.title: D: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct sentence.

items.sentence: I wish I would have more money.
items.answer: I wish I had more money.

items.sentence: She wishes she would be taller.
items.answer: She wishes she was taller./She wishes she were taller.

items.sentence: I wish you will stop interrupting me.
items.answer: I wish you would stop interrupting me.

items.sentence: They wish they saw the match last night.
items.answer: They wish they had seen the match last night.

items.sentence: I wish I could passed the test yesterday.
items.answer: I wish I had passed the test yesterday.

items.sentence: I wish it doesn't rain tomorrow.
items.answer: I hope it doesn't rain tomorrow.

sections.title: E: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word given.

items.context: I don't have a car, so I can't drive to work.
items.sentence: I wish I ...... a car so I could drive to work. **HAD**

items.context: It's a pity she didn't study harder for the test.
items.sentence: She wishes she ...... harder for the test. **STUDIED**

items.context: I'm not good at drawing and I regret it.
items.sentence: I wish I ...... at drawing. **WERE**

items.context: The neighbours make too much noise and it annoys me.
items.sentence: I wish my neighbours ...... so much noise. **MAKE**

items.context: I can't swim and I wish I could.
items.sentence: If only I ...... swim. **COULD**

items.context: We stayed out too late and now we're tired.
items.sentence: We wish we ...... out so late last night. **STAYED**
